Home › Districts › Mitte
Centre · Central 11 editor's picks

Hotels in Mitte

Mitte is Berlin's historic and geographic heart — Brandenburg Gate, Museum Island, Reichstag and Unter den Linden all within a few square kilometers. Stay here to be right next to Berlin's iconic landmarks and at the center of cultural life.

Brandenburg Gate Museum Island Reichstag Gendarmenmarkt Unter den Linden TV Tower
How the district feels

Atmosphere

Stately, history-rich, urban-elegant. Tourists by day, theatre and fine dining by night.

Ideal for

Who stays here right

  • ✓ First-time visitors
  • ✓ Luxury travellers
  • ✓ Culture fans
  • ✓ Short stays

The grande dame of Berlin hotels, right on Brandenburg Gate.

Mitte

Hotel de Rome

Rocco Forte's Berlin flagship on Bebelplatz, in the former Dresdner Bank.

from €420/night

Mitte

Five-star property on Gendarmenmarkt with the Charlotte & Fritz restaurant.

Mitte

from €145/night

Soho House Berlin

from €320/night

from €290/night

The Circus Hotel

from €95/night

Strong presence

Popular hotel styles in Mitte

✦

Luxury Hotels

5-star, concierge service, spa, Michelin restaurant. Especially in Mitte and Charlottenburg.

from €280/night hotels
◆

Boutique Hotels

Small, individual, design-driven. Berlin has one of Europe's densest boutique hotel scenes.

from €140/night hotels
▣

Business Hotels

Conference rooms, fast Wi-Fi, breakfast from 6am, good transport links.

from €130/night hotels
♨

Wellness & Spa Hotels

Sauna, pool, massage. In the Grunewald, by Wannsee, by Müggelsee.

from €170/night hotels
→

Hotels with Shuttle

Transfers to/from BER, trade fair or stations — ideal for late-night arrivals.

from €100/night hotels
◇

Design Hotels

Architecturally composed houses — Berlin has been a design hotel capital since the 2000s.

from €160/night hotels
▢

Apartment Hotels

With kitchen and living area — ideal for longer stays and families.

from €95/night hotels
Nearby

Attractions in Mitte

The key landmarks, museums and parks easily reached from your hotel in Mitte.

Thomas Wolf, www.foto-tw.de · CC BY-SA 3.0
Landmark · Mitte

Brandenburg Gate

Berlin's iconic landmark — symbol of reunification. Right on Pariser Platz, flanked by the Adlon and the Holocaust Memorial.

Mfield, Matthew Field, http://www.photography.mattfield.com; edit by Waugsberg (rotation 0,4°) · GFDL 1.2
Landmark · Mitte

Reichstag

Seat of the German Bundestag with the famous Norman Foster glass dome. Pre-registration recommended to visit the dome.

Thomas Wolf, www.foto-tw.de · CC BY-SA 3.0
Museum · Mitte

Museum Island

UNESCO World Heritage site with five world-class museums: Pergamon, Neues Museum, Altes Museum, Alte Nationalgalerie, Bode-Museum.

Tobi85 · CC BY-SA 4.0
Viewpoint · Mitte

TV Tower Alexanderplatz

At 368 m the tallest building in Germany. Rotating restaurant and observation deck — the best view over all of Berlin.

Roger Wollstadt · CC BY-SA 2.0
History · Mitte

Checkpoint Charlie

Most famous Cold War border crossing. Today an open-air exhibition with the Mauermuseum right next door.

beedubz · CC BY-SA 3.0
Park · Mitte

Tiergarten

Berlin's central city park — 210 hectares between Brandenburg Gate and Zoo Station. Victory Column, English Garden, Café am Neuen See.

Marek Heise Fotografie, Berlin · CC BY-SA 4.0
Landmark · Mitte

Gendarmenmarkt

Arguably Berlin's most beautiful square — Deutscher Dom, Französischer Dom and the Konzerthaus form a perfect ensemble.

Avda · CC BY-SA 3.0
Landmark · Mitte

Potsdamer Platz

Modern Berlin distilled — Sony Center, Berlinale Palace, towers by Renzo Piano and Helmut Jahn. Completely rebuilt after reunification.

Alexander Blum · CC BY-SA 4.0
History · Mitte

Holocaust Memorial

Peter Eisenman's field of stelae between Brandenburg Gate and Potsdamer Platz — one of Europe's most important memorials.

Taxiarchos228 · FAL
Landmark · Mitte

Alexanderplatz

Berlin's central transit hub — World Clock, TV Tower, Galeria department store. Everything comes together here.

Ansgar Koreng · CC BY 3.0 de
Landmark · Mitte

Berlin Cathedral

Right on Museum Island — main Hohenzollern church, with an impressive dome and view over the Lustgarten.

Keep exploring

Other Berlin districts

All 12 districts
Dietmar Rabich · CC BY-SA 4.0
East 4 hotels

Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg

Alternative, creative, vibrant. Nightlife on the edge, street art on every corner, cafés open late.

East Side Gallery Mercedes-Benz Arena RAW area
Fridolin freudenfett · CC BY-SA 3.0
West 9 hotels

Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf

Elegant, refined, calm. Shopping on Ku'damm, coffee at Savignyplatz, classical concerts at the Philharmonie.

Kurfürstendamm Charlottenburg Palace KaDeWe
nikey · CC BY-SA 4.0
North 4 hotels

Pankow

Family-friendly, green, relaxed. Cafés with kids' corners, Sundays at Mauerpark, stuccoed period buildings.

Mauerpark Kollwitzplatz Kulturbrauerei
Reinhold Möller Ermell · CC BY-SA 4.0
South 2 hotels

Neukölln

Hip, diverse, creative, young. Brunch spots, late-night kiosks, bargain markets, gallery openings.

Tempelhofer Feld Maybachufer Klunkerkranich
